summary.intent: Fix multiple bugs in the Gossamer VS Code extension — a tool that indexes and displays Claude Code sessions — including server lifecycle issues, database schema problems, multi-repo DB client architecture, session discovery delays, and various UI improvements.
summary.outcome: Resolved all reported issues across the session: server port conflict handling, per-repo DB clients, checkpoint indexing with correct authors/sorting, live session detection from .entire/metadata/, agent-specific colors, markdown+highlight coexistence, and several UI layout improvements.

repo: pushSchema split SQL on ';\n' then filtered chunks starting with '--', but every Prisma-generated statement starts with '-- CreateTable', so all 56 statements were silently discarded on every startup
The server had a single 'db'/'dbPath' primary client variable that most endpoints used; the correct architecture is a dbRegistry Map with allDbs()/dbForRepo() helpers since each repo is equal

Shadow branches (entire/<session-id>) are only committed at checkpoint boundaries — potentially minutes into a session; live transcript is written immediately to .entire/metadata/<sessionId>/full.jsonl
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr on SQLite files causes disk I/O errors; affected DBs need to be deleted and recreated (evictDb + unlink)

gitlog npm package defaults to nameStatus: true, adding --name-status to git log; on large repos (cosmos-graphql with 9000+ commits) this exceeds Node's maxBuffer, throwing silently inside a catch block
Git worktree add fails with 'branch already used by worktree' when two local repos share the same entire/checkpoints/v1 branch; fix is --detach
The checkpoint branch fetch was shallow (.git/shallow contained the branch tip), limiting git rev-list to 1 commit; unshallowing revealed 203 commits
Bare git repos store fetched refs at refs/heads/* not refs/remotes/origin/*, unlike normal clones
Session sorting by localPath cwd broke cross-machine comparison since the same repo lives at different paths; sorting by git remote is machine-independent
gitUserName fell back to the local machine's git config user when the DB value was null, mis-attributing other users' sessions to the current user

MarkdownView had HighlightTermsCtx threading through inline renderer, but this broke markdown parsing for nodes containing match terms; post-render DOM walking with TreeWalker decouples highlighting from parsing entirely

code.finding: pushSchema only ran on the primary DB at startup; secondary repo DBs were never initialized with schema
httpServer.listen called without an 'error' event handler, so EADDRINUSE caused a silent crash
spawnServer guard only checked in-memory GossamerPanel.server static; orphaned processes from previous VS Code sessions held the port but reset the static
findCommitForCheckpoint used gitlog with nameStatus: true (default), causing maxBuffer exceeded on large repos; fix: nameStatus: false + execOptions.maxBuffer: 100MB
cpUser?.gitUserName ?? gitUser.name fell back to local user's git config when checkpoint author was null/empty — silently mis-attributes sessions
EventItem rendered without user prop, always showing 'You'; session.gitUserName was available from session object but never passed down
HighlightTermsCtx threaded through inline parser broke markdown formatting when match terms overlapped with markdown syntax; replaced with post-render DOM TreeWalker approach
toolsExpanded/thinkingExpanded initialized to false; search hits inside collapsed sections were unreachable — must initialize to true when any tool/thinking block contains a match term
GET /api/sessions used qLocalPath only for sorting (not filtering), but sort used localPath cwd which breaks cross-machine comparison; fixed to sort by matching git remote instead
catch block in saveGitOidMapping swallows all errors silently, including the maxBuffer overflow that caused zero CheckpointIdGitOidJoin rows

workflow: When a Prisma-generated schema.sql is split by semicolons and filtered by comment prefix, all chunks will be dropped because Prisma prepends '-- CreateTable' to every statement
Post-render DOM walking (TreeWalker + splitText) is the correct approach for search highlighting over rendered HTML — inline-parser approaches break formatting for edge cases
fs.watch on macOS can miss cross-process file writes; a fast poll (every 2-3s) as a fallback is necessary alongside the watcher
bare git clones store fetched refs directly at refs/heads/* without a remotes/ namespace; fetch refspec must be +refs/heads/*:refs/heads/* explicitly
SQLite files on macOS can acquire com.apple.provenance xattr that blocks I/O; defensive DB retry logic (evict cache, delete file, recreate) handles this transparently
lsof -ti tcp:<port> provides a reliable way to find and kill orphaned processes holding a port before spawning a replacement

friction: pushSchema bug caused 'no such table' errors on every startup; required multiple debugging sessions because the SQL file, regex, and Prisma client all looked correct in isolation — the filter was only wrong in context
gitlog maxBuffer overflow was caught silently inside saveGitOidMapping's catch block, producing 0 CheckpointIdGitOidJoin rows with no visible error — required manual debug script to discover
macOS com.apple.provenance xattr on .db files caused 'disk I/O error' that was opaque until running sqlite3 directly on the file
The Entire CLI has stopped writing to shadow branches and full.jsonl for cosmos-graphql for 3+ weeks with no visible error — diagnosis was inconclusive from Gossamer's side
Shallow git clone of the checkpoint branch made git rev-list return 1 commit, masking the full history and confusing ancestry checks
The user had to correct the Homebrew tap name (entire/tap vs entireio/tap) after the assistant used the wrong one
open_items: Checkpoint remote support (bare clone + worktree from ~/.gossamer/checkpoint-repos/) is implemented but not tested end-to-end with an actual Entire.io checkpoint remote
Cross-node search term matches in markdown are still not highlighted — only matches contained within a single text node are found by the TreeWalker approach
Entire CLI is not writing full.jsonl or updating shadow branches in cosmos-graphql (no activity for 3+ weeks); root cause not identified — may be a dead background process

CheckpointIdGitOidJoin population still depends on findCommitForCheckpoint which searches main/master for Entire-Checkpoint: trailers; checkpoints from contributors whose commits aren't locally reachable will have no OID mapping

GET /api/sessions/:id and checkpoint endpoints accept ?localPath= for DB resolution, but the webview doesn't always have the session's localPath at call time — currently falls back to probing all DBs which works but is slower
The /api/repos/current endpoint was updated to accept ?localPath= but the underlying repo config matching could still mis-match if remotes differ in SSH vs HTTPS format
